content.full_text:
May 16, 1990

Jonathan Rothman, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Jonathan:

This letter is sent to request written confirmation of telephone advice you provided this office last week concerning the requirements of Proposition 105 (Gov. Code Sections 84501-84514). We request this advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) on behalf of our clients "Californians for Transportation Solutions sponsored by the Planning and Conservation League -- Yes on Proposition 116" ("Committee").

The Committee planned to purchase lawn signs which would promote the passage of Proposition 116. You advised me that the Proposition 105 requirements applied to lawn signs and, therefore, the signs had to include a disclosure statement regarding major funding sources to the Committee. You further advised me that the requirement in Government Code Section 84509 that the disclosure statement is to be printed "clearly and legibly in a conspicuous manner" meant that the disclosure statement on the lawn signs had to be "visible from the street;" the statement did not have to be the same size nor did it have to be as clear or legible as the message on the lawn sign.

With respect to the application of the definition of "industry" in Government Code Section 84504, you indicated that real estate lawyers, real estate developers, and real estate brokers should be treated as three separate industries. Thus the contributions from these three groups would be added separately for purposes of determining whether their contributions exceeded the levels set forth in the definition of "major funding source" (Gov. Code Section 84507).

Jonathan Rothman  
May 16, 1990  
Page Two

You also indicated to me that neither the Commission nor the staff have established rules or guidelines on the required content or format of the disclosure statement. While you said that the term "major funding" should be included in the disclosure statement, you did not specify any other specific format requirements. You also stated that the statement could be accompanied by additional language (such as "other funding sources include ...") so long as the additional language did not interfere with or otherwise distort the message of the disclosure required by Proposition 105.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[Signature]
DIANE M. RISHBURN

DMF:sk
